name: disk
explanation: The Disk check is a host-local system check with no required configuration fields and no remote target. It enumerates partitions/devices via `psutil.disk_partitions()` and `psutil.disk_usage()` on the Agent host, optionally calls `blkid`/`lsblk` for labels (Linux) or creates SMB/NFS mounts (Windows). All instance options are filters/tagging knobs over the locally-discovered set. Auto-config is trivial: emit the integration with the default empty instance on every Agent host (the check is in fact enabled by default in core Agent). Discovery is universal — every host has at least the root filesystem — and there is no network probe. This places it alongside btrfs/iis as a host-presence check.
notes: Generic with method `other` (no on-wire probe). Trigger is universal host presence — like btrfs but unconditional. The optional `create_mounts` Windows branch carries SMB/NFS user/password, but that is opt-in; default-mode auto-config does not need credentials.
